learning_objectives: Define key terminology
Identify and assist patients/residents with daily routine care, promoting independence and dignity
Identify benefits of bathing and list five areas of body that require bathing daily
Describe the procedure for bathing patients/residents and safety guidelines
List purposes and schedule for oral hygiene
Describe procedure of oral hygiene and denture care for a patient/resident needing assistance, and for the unconscious individual
Identify the Nurse Assistant role and responsibility for patient/resident nail care
Identify Nurse Assistant role and responsibility for caring for hair of patients/residents
Describe procedure for shaving a patient/resident
List ways to maintain healthy skin and identify at risk patients.
Describe causes, signs and symptoms, and staging of pressure sores.
Describe general nursing measures to prevent and treat pressure sores
Describe general guidelines for selecting and caring for patient's/resident's clothing
Discuss rationale for use of street clothes and guidelines for dressing or undressing a patient/resident
Describe the usual frequency pattern for urination
List the observations to be made about urine
Describe urinary incontinence and the need for immediate care
Describe the purpose and general rules of care for urinary catheters
Describe the goals and methods for bowel and bladder training
Describe the normal pattern of bowel movements and reportable observations
Explain the purpose of an ostomy and the care of patients/residents with an ostomy
Describe the procedures for weighing and measuring height of the patient/resident
Describe common prosthetic devices and their care (artificial limbs, contact lenses, dentures, eyeglasses and hearing aids)
Describe the application, removal and care for "behind the ear" hearing aids
